title: ribes spicatum subsp. pubescens
intro: Ribes spicatum subsp. pubescens, also known as the prickly currant or alpine currant, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the currant family (Grossulariaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of phenolic compounds and anthocyanins. The health benefits of Ribes spicatum subsp. pubescens include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including colds, fever, and digestive issues.
